title: diploblechnum lenormandii
intro: Diploblechnum lenormandii, also known as the 'Māori fern', is a species of fern that is native to New Zealand. It has been used in traditional Māori medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant is said to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, treating respiratory issues, and aiding in the digestion process. Diploblechnum lenormandii can be prepared into various herbal remedies, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
